data.title: Algonquin Power to sell renewable energy business to LS Power for up to $2.5B
data.description:
Algonquin Power & Utilities (NYSE:AQN) said Friday it agreed to sell its renewable energy business, excluding hydro, to LS Power for up to $2.5B.

The deal consists of $2.28B of cash at closing and up to $220M of cash tied to an earn-out agreement relating to certain wind assets.

The sale, combined with the agreement supportiing the sale of Atlantica shares, "delivers on our plan to transform AQN into a pure play regulated utility, optimize our regulated business activities, strengthen our balance sheet, and enhance our quality of earnings," CEO Chris Huskilson said.

Algonquin Power (AQN) also released Q2 results, showing a slight beat in adjusted earnings while missing on revenues.

data.title: Confluent stock rises as J.P. Morgan upgrades to Overweight
data.description:
Confluent (NASDAQ:CFLT) shares were up 4.2% in premarket trade, as J.P. Morgan upgraded the company to Overweight on the firm's organizational resilience.

"Having seen the organizational resilience over the last couple of quarters, with no discernible disruption, in addition to the recent valuation reset driven by the choppy macro environment, we are now comfortable moving into an Overweight position, with a medium-term view," the analysts said.

JPM added its conviction was further bolstered by its latest Confluent customer survey, which indicated that the consumers were looking to grow their spend at a 20% CAGR over the next three years, on average.

"Given all the growth unlocks coming online over the next year, we think there is a high likelihood for the overall growth glidepath to inflect higher, toward at least a mid-to-high 20s% level, sometime in 2025 and into 2026," JPM added.

The analysts added, that while the dynamic macro backdrop remains a bit of a wildcard and could continue to influence results in the very near term, they see a solid risk/reward at the current price levels for long term investors.

In July, the company beat both its top and bottom line estimates for the second quarter.

SA quant rates the company at Hold, while SA analysts rate it at Buy.

Shares in the company were down 14.8% since the start of the year.

data.title: Savers Value Village Non-GAAP EPS of $0.14
data.description:
• Savers Value Village press release (NYSE:SVV): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.14.
  • Net sales increased 2% to $386.7 million, with the United States up 5.4% and Canada down 2.4%.
  • Net income and adjusted net income were $9.7 million and $23.7 million, respectively.
  • Total active members enrolled in our U.S. and Canadian loyalty programs increased 11.8% to 5.7 million.

data.title: Dividend Roundup: Prudential Financial, Progressive, Alcoa, Eli Lilly, and more
data.description:
This week's dividend activity included increased payouts from J&J Snacks (JJSF) and C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) as well as declarations from companies like Progressive (NYSE:PGR) and Prudential Financial (NYSE:PRU).

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Alcoa (NYSE:AA) and Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Dividend Increases

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend increase in the past week:

EnerSys (ENS)

  • Dividend declared: August 7
  • New dividend amount: $0.24
  • Previous amount: $0.225
  • Increase: 6.7%

J&J Snacks (JJSF)

  • Dividend declared: August 7
  • New dividend amount: $0.78
  • Previous amount: $0.735
  • Increase: 6.1%

C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW)

  • Dividend declared: August 8
  • New dividend amount: $0.62
  • Previous amount: $0.61
  • Increase: 1.6%

Dividend Declarations

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend in the past week:

Progressive (PGR)

  • Dividend declared: August 5
  • Dividend amount: $0.10

Prudential Financial (PRU)

  • Dividend declared: August 6
  • Dividend amount: $1.30

Shutterstock (SSTK)

  • Dividend declared: August 6
  • Dividend amount: $0.30

Hilton Worldwide (HLT)

  • Dividend declared: August 7
  • Dividend amount: $0.15

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Here are some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week:

Alcoa (AA)

  • Ex-dividend date: August 12
  • Payout date: August 29

ConocoPhillips (COP)

  • Ex-dividend date: August 12
  • Payout date: September 3

Eli Lilly (LLY)

  • Ex-dividend date: August 15
  • Payout date: September 10

Diamondback Energy (FANG)

  • Ex-dividend date: August 15
  • Payout date: August 22
